geometric_interpretation.claim_boundary: I5-A strengthens replay-backed SU3 evidence only for delayed and mild-peer-flux preservation. Repeated re-entry is a fail-closed depletion boundary. I5-A does not support durable SU4, transfer SU5, SU6, final N22, the N21 ND6 bridge, semantic learning, choice, agency, native support, sentience, Phase 8, or ant-ecology implementation.
geometric_interpretation.short_read: I5-A shows the replay-backed SU3 candidates preserve their route-specific delta under delayed re-entry and mild peer flux, while repeated re-entry exposes a fail-closed depletion boundary.
geometric_interpretation.what_changed_from_i5: I5 tested clean replay. I5-A keeps the same thresholds and starts from the saved post-interaction state, then delays re-entry, repeats re-entry, or injects a mild unrelated peer flux before route_b re-entry. The second repeated re-entry depletes the route-specific delta below the SU3 preservation ratio, so I5-A records limited replay-stress support rather than SU4 evidence.

purpose: replay durability stress over I5 SU3 candidates
